differential_diagnoses: Cushing syndrome (adrenocortical carcinoma or ectopic ACTH)
Adrenal insufficiency (secondary to rapid withdrawal or occult etiology)
next_steps: Initiate empiric treatment for bacterial meningitis, including coverage for Listeria (ampicillin)
Obtain infectious disease consultation for specialized CSF testing (India ink, fungal culture, AFB, PCR for TB/fungi)
Perform hormonal workup (ACTH, cortisol, dexamethasone suppression test, metanephrines, DHEAS)
Measure serum and CSF lactate to further differentiate bacterial vs. other etiologies
Re-evaluate for occult malignancy or paraneoplastic syndromes
Immediate empiric treatment with vancomycin, ceftriaxone, ampicillin, and liposomal amphotericin B
Send CSF for Tropheryma whipplei PCR and fungal/bacterial/TB cultures and PCR
Perform CSF cytology and flow cytometry for malignancy
Send serum and CSF for paraneoplastic antibody panels
Perform full endocrine workup (ACTH, cortisol, DHEAS, plasma metanephrines) for adrenal lesion
Consult neurology for repeat imaging or biopsy if CSF studies are non-diagnostic
Monitor intracranial pressure and provide neuro-protective supportive care
